mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 351561M as of 2005-10-01 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods produce negative SPX returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and remains elevated for 2+ months, it signals restrictive monetary policy that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward equity returns over the next 60 trading days. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when the tightening regime is sufficiently entrenched.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% as of 2005-10-01 is above historical median
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and corporate hiring expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction between labor supply contraction and demand weakness amplifies the negative signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25pp month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% and PAYEMS at 134061K as of 2005-10-01
failure_condition: Joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 3-month moving average signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This is a high-frequency trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.055 (vintage) / 95.3005 (revised) as of 2005-09-01 shows measurement volatility
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods produce positive 5-day returns in < 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) interact with real GDP growth (GDPC1) such that strong housing construction combined with above-trend GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand and forward earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand components to be simultaneously strong.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 2000K units annualized AND GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2108K (vintage) / 2151K (revised) as of 2005-09-01 reflects strong housing market
failure_condition: Joint strength in HOUST and GDPC1 produces positive 60-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy pressure and expectations of future rate hikes, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when inflation breaches the tolerance band.
failure_condition: CPI > 2.5% produces negative 20-day returns in < 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window in high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month, signaling weakening labor demand and reduced consumer income growth, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This is a regime-conditional signal active only in late-cycle environments.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth < 100K produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously decline month-over-month, signaling aggregate demand weakness that reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction amplifies the recessionary signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11193.2B (vintage) / 16047.587B (revised) shows significant revision risk
failure_condition: Joint weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS produces negative 20-day returns in < 52% of triggered instances, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
